intended_use.primary_use: Automated scoring of credit card transactions for fraud risk in a real-time decision support pipeline
out_of_scope: Sole basis for financial crime prosecution
Transactions outside the credit card domain

training_data.dataset: Kaggle Credit Card Fraud Dataset (ULB Machine Learning Group)
preprocessing: TF-IDF + SVD on synthetic transaction memos (NLP pipeline)

decision_threshold.strategy: Grid search maximising F1-score on validation set

calibration.note: Applied after XGBoost training to produce well-calibrated probabilities

model_architecture.class_imbalance_handling: scale_pos_weight (ratio of negatives to positives, computed per fold)

bias_and_fairness: The dataset contains PCA-anonymised features — demographic information (race, gender, age) is not directly accessible
The model has not been evaluated for disparate impact across demographic groups due to data anonymisation
Decisions based on this model should be reviewed by human analysts before adverse action
privacy: All input features are anonymised PCA components — raw transaction data is not stored
Audit logs store a SHA-256 hash of inputs, not the inputs themselves
The system complies with data minimisation principles
transparency: SHAP explanations are available per-prediction via /explain endpoint
Business rules are fully documented in configs/business_rules.yaml
All training code and configurations are version-controlled and reproducible

limitations: Perfect scores on this dataset are known and documented in the ML community — the dataset is highly separable by tree-based models due to its PCA preprocessing
The model was trained on 2013 European transaction data and may not generalise to modern fraud patterns without retraining
Concept drift in fraud patterns should be monitored continuously using the built-in Evidently integration
The NLP memo features are synthetic and not derived from real transaction descriptions
Class imbalance (0.17% fraud) means the model is optimised for recall — expect some false positives in production

monitoring_and_maintenance.audit_trail: All predictions logged to SQLite (data/audit_log.db) with full provenance
monitoring_and_maintenance.retraining_trigger: Recommend retraining when drift_share > 20% or monthly
